name: TIME SMEAR - slit-scan temporal displacement reel where each column shows a different moment
ref: Codrops/Coding Train slit-scan time-displacement (thecodingtrain.com/challenges/164-slitscan); GLSL real-time slitscan shader from scratch (Feb 2026, derivative.ca community tutorial)
palette: #0b0b0d (void) + #f0ece4 (bone HUD/labels) + #e8472a (vermilion scan-edge) + #2c6fad (cobalt past-frame tint); media supplies the streaked color
brief: Signature interaction: each work is a short looping clip rendered to a Canvas 2D ring-buffer of recent frames, and the displayed frame samples a DIFFERENT moment per column (column x reads frame now minus x), so motion smears into the iconic time-warped streak; cursor X bends the smear gradient steeper or shallower, click freezes the buffer. Distinct from everything built because no template does TEMPORAL column sampling: liquid-distortion (WebGL displacement) and noise-displacement-hover (Perlin push) are SPATIAL pixel offsets within one instant, glitch-datamosh is RGB-split/block tearing, depth-map-tilt is per-pixel depth parallax; none read across a frame history buffer. Renders at scroll=0 statically: the hero work is seeded with a pre-filled buffer so a fully-formed streak shows on the no-interaction screenshot (do not start from an empty buffer that paints flat). Mobile fallback: a single pre-baked streak still image (no live video decode/buffer) with the HUD, contained and letterboxed at <=640px. Perf guardrail: source video drawn to a small offscreen canvas (cap ~320px wide), ring buffer capped at ~120 frames, one rAF, paused on document.hidden and fully gated under prefers-reduced-motion (freeze on a rich frame). Canvas 2D only, NO WebGL; any inline SVG HUD ticks use hex fill/stroke literals NOT var() in presentation attributes. Native CSS/JS only, NO GSAP or external libs.

name: DUST FORM - a single GPU point-cloud holds one work's silhouette then morphs into the next
ref: Three.js Journey 'Particles Morphing Shader' (threejs-journey.com/lessons/particles-morphing-shader); Codrops 'Crafting a Dreamy Particle Effect with Three.js and GPGPU' (tympanus.net/codrops, particle image-formation lineage)
palette: #06060a (void) + #e8f0ff (cool dust) + #ff9f3f (amber ignite during morph) + #f0ece4 (bone label); the formed image supplies all recognizable color
brief: Signature interaction: one persistent Three.js point-cloud (~30k points) is positioned by sampling each work image's luminance to a target buffer, so the particles literally ASSEMBLE into that work's silhouette; scroll (or click-next) lerps every point from the current image's target to the next image's target, the cloud dispersing through an amber-ignited dust state mid-transition then re-forming. Distinct from data-physics-particle-sort (separate per-work clouds that SORT into data formations, never a single cloud morphing image-A to image-B), particle-constellation (static node star-map with link lines, no morph), and page-morph-barba (full-scene shader dissolve between routes, not a point-cloud forming images). Renders at scroll=0 statically: the cloud is seeded fully FORMED into work 1's recognizable silhouette at rest (not mid-disperse soup), so the thumbnail reads as an image made of particles. Mobile fallback: particle count dropped to ~10k and morph driven by tap-next instead of scroll; contain + letterbox. WebGL guardrail: ANGLE returns 0 for GLSL ES 1.0 dynamic-indexed local arrays, so palette/threshold ramps go through a LUT texture not an indexed local array; preserveDrawingBuffer:true so the headless thumbnail captures the painted canvas; confirm the canvas actually paints one frame before sign-off; DPR capped at 1, additive blending, single rAF, gated under prefers-reduced-motion (static formed cloud). Three.js via importmap; NO GSAP or external libs.

name: LIQUID MENU - SVG goo-threshold blobs of media physically fuse and split as they drift
ref: Codrops 'Creative Gooey Effects' + SVG metaball feGaussianBlur/feColorMatrix threshold (tympanus.net/codrops, svg filter tag); animationpatterns.art gooey-blob-metaball-filter (2026)
palette: #0d0d10 (void) + #f0ece4 (bone) + #c94a2e (vermilion active blob) + #3ab8a8 (teal merge-glow); each blob clips a real work thumbnail
brief: Signature interaction: 6 to 9 work thumbnails live inside circular blobs that drift on slow orbits through ONE shared SVG goo filter (feGaussianBlur then a feColorMatrix alpha-threshold), so whenever two blobs touch their edges fuse into a single liquid mass with a teal bridge, then split apart again as they separate; hovering a blob pulls it toward the cursor and swells it, click locks it full-bleed. Distinct from generative-svg-filter (feTurbulence/feDisplacementMap emulsion surface-warp on static media, no blob topology), voronoi-cell-gallery (hard-edged tessellation that never merges), and infinite-canvas (rectangular cards on a pan/zoom board, no fusing geometry). Renders at scroll=0 statically: at rest the blobs are arranged so at least one pair is mid-fuse (a visible liquid bridge) on the no-interaction screenshot, making the metaball grammar unmistakable without motion. Mobile fallback: blobs freeze into a static fused cluster (drift off), tap to expand; contain + letterbox at <=640px. Perf guardrail: the goo filter runs on a single small filter region, blob count capped at 9, drift on one rAF that pauses on document.hidden and freezes under prefers-reduced-motion. The SVG filter primitives and any blob fill/stroke use hex literals NOT a CSS var() in presentation attributes (var() in an SVG presentation attribute silently fails); media is referenced via clipPath/pattern not a paint var. No canvas, no WebGL. Native CSS/SVG/JS only, NO GSAP or external libs.
